The calculator provides a free online version with the option of combining name and date or using only the name to calculate the chart. The result can be used to reflect on one's actions and behaviors, without being forced into a predetermined pattern. By examining the areas highlighted in each tarot card, users can gain insight into their strengths and weaknesses, and make more informed decisions about their lives.

articles.title: Flux 3
articles.summary: FLUX 3 is now available in Early Access and represents a significant advancement in multimodal flow models, which jointly learn from images, videos, and audio within a unified architecture. This model learns to represent the world by capturing spatial structures and relationships, temporal dynamics, physical laws, and causal relationships between mechanical phenomena and acoustics. FLUX 3's capabilities include text-to-video generation, image-to-video generation, video-to-video generation from reference clips, and more, all of which are evidence that it can perceive, predict, and act across physical and digital environments.

articles.summary: The WebAIM Million report found that 56.1 million distinct accessibility errors were detected across the top 1,000,000 web pages, with an average of 56.1 errors per page. This represents a 10.1% increase from the previous year's analysis in 2025, which found 51 errors per page. The report highlights the need for developers to prioritize accessibility and provide training, testing, and consulting services to ensure that their websites are usable by individuals with disabilities.

articles.title: Making GIFs from 35mm film photography
articles.summary: Everyone loves a good GIF, and my favourite (of all time) is this one circa 2015: Let’s make a GIF in the hardest way possible. Why? It’s pointless, but some pointless things are just fun to do. Step 1 Acquire photons using the Lomo ActionSampler , a plastic camera made in China by anonymous unknown factories. The camera uses a unique clockwork spring-powered mechanism to rotate a circular shutter over 4 different lenses at a speed of around 1/250s per frame, capturing 1 second of action at 4 frames per second. Developing film is necessary to make the photos visible, and it can be done at home with parts like developer tanks and reels. The resulting images are then scanned using an LED light panel and digital camera on a tripod for optimal detail.

articles.summary: Garbage In, Memory Safety Out is a topic of significant interest for developers who work with memory safety in JavaScript. The Fil-C: Garbage In, Memory Safety Out experiment aims to demonstrate the importance of garbage collection and memory management in preventing crashes and improving performance. articles.title: Jolt: running Clojure on Chez Scheme
articles.summary: Jolt: running Clojure on Chez Scheme is a project aimed at creating a drop-in replacement for JVM Clojure, offering a fast startup and light memory footprint. The use of Chez Scheme as the underlying language allows for seamless core semantics mapping to Scheme, while also leveraging its mature JIT compiler and generational garbage collector. This approach enables native binaries with instant startup times on a wide range of operating systems, making it an attractive alternative to the Java runtime.

articles.title: Everyone Should Know SIMD
articles.summary: SIMD can be simple to understand and is not just for high-performance software, but also useful in everyday programming. It involves processing multiple values in parallel by comparing or performing arithmetic across all lanes of a single instruction, resulting in localized speedups that directly map to the CPU's parallelism. This common pattern can be applied to loops iterating over large numbers of bytes, leading to significant performance gains when done correctly.

articles.title: Why Software Factories Fail (or: harness engineering is not enough)
articles.summary: Software factories that claim to revolutionize coding by automating everything often fail due to underlying issues such as lack of human oversight and expertise. While loop engineering may seem like a key technology for streamlining development processes, the reality is that relying solely on automated tools can lead to code quality problems and increased risk of errors. As companies rush into production with AI-powered software factories, they must be aware of these limitations and take steps to ensure their systems are robust and reliable.
